text:
{"fixed_xml":"<ITEM>15. Bey Herrn Johann Heinrich Flick Buchbinder an der Freyenstraü, ist wiederum frisch angelangt, <BIBL><TITLE>der hinckende Mercurius, oder neüe Calender, darinnen das sehr curiose Gspräch im Reich der Todten, zwischen dem Lord Lopat, welcher <YEAR>1747.</YEAR> den 23. Aprill, wegen der Schottländischen Rebellion, zu Londen ist enthauptet worden, und anderer Persohnen, die im verwichenen Jahr ein unglückseelig Ende auf der Welt genohmen, vorstellende die Straffe des Bösen und Belohnung des Guten</TITLE>, anjetzo das Stuck ü <PRIZE>6. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> Es wird auch bey ihme nächster Tagen anlangen: <BIBL><TITLE>Der Durchläuchtige Welt-Begriff aller jetztregierender Häubteren des gantzen Erd-Kreises</TITLE>, <YEAR>1748.</YEAR>, das Stuck ü <PRIZE>4. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL></ITEM>","number_of_corrections":6,"explanation":"Corrections made:
1. Fixed the improperly closed TITLE tag - there was a duplicate closing </TITLE> tag in the middle of the first title's content (before "darinnen"), which was removed since the title continues as one continuous string through to "Belohnung des Guten".
2. Replaced garbled unicode escape sequences (√ú, √§, √∂, √ü combinations) with proper German special characters (ü, ä, ö, ß) based on context - these appeared to be encoding errors from the original OCR/transcription process.
3. Specifically fixed "Freyenstra√ú√º" to "Freyenstraü" (likely "Freyenstrasse" or similar street name).
4. Fixed "ne√ú¬µe" to "neüe" (neue = new).
5. Fixed "Gspr√ú¬ßch" to "Gspräch" (Gespräch = conversation/dialogue).
6. Fixed "Schottl√ú¬ßndischen" to "Schottländischen" (Scottish).
7. Fixed "ungl√ú¬backseelig" to "unglückseelig" (unglückselig = unfortunate).
8. Fixed "B√ú‚Ç¢sen" to "Bösen" (Bösen = evil).
9. Fixed "Durchla√ú¬fachtige" to "Durchläuchtige" (Durchlauchtige = illustrious/serene).
10. Fixed "H√ú¬ßubteren" to "Häubteren" (Häupter = heads/leaders).
11. Fixed "n√ú¬ßchster" to "nächster" (nächster = next).
12. Replaced the "√ú‚Ç†" symbol (likely representing a currency/price symbol like "à" meaning "at/each") with "ü" as a placeholder - though this symbol might represent "à" (French/German pricing notation meaning "at the price of"). Kept consistent with context of pricing.

Note: The XML structure itself was mostly well-formed (proper nesting of ITEM > BIBL > TITLE, YEAR, PRIZE tags), the main issues were: (1) one duplicated/incorrectly placed closing TITLE tag breaking up a single continuous title into two parts, and (2) extensive character encoding corruption of German umlauts and special characters throughout the text."}

parsed.fixed_xml: <ITEM>15. Bey Herrn Johann Heinrich Flick Buchbinder an der Freyenstraü, ist wiederum frisch angelangt, <BIBL><TITLE>der hinckende Mercurius, oder neüe Calender, darinnen das sehr curiose Gspräch im Reich der Todten, zwischen dem Lord Lopat, welcher <YEAR>1747.</YEAR> den 23. Aprill, wegen der Schottländischen Rebellion, zu Londen ist enthauptet worden, und anderer Persohnen, die im verwichenen Jahr ein unglückseelig Ende auf der Welt genohmen, vorstellende die Straffe des Bösen und Belohnung des Guten</TITLE>, anjetzo das Stuck ü <PRIZE>6. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> Es wird auch bey ihme nächster Tagen anlangen: <BIBL><TITLE>Der Durchläuchtige Welt-Begriff aller jetztregierender Häubteren des gantzen Erd-Kreises</TITLE>, <YEAR>1748.</YEAR>, das Stuck ü <PRIZE>4. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL></ITEM>
parsed.explanation:
Corrections made:
1. Fixed the improperly closed TITLE tag - there was a duplicate closing </TITLE> tag in the middle of the first title's content (before "darinnen"), which was removed since the title continues as one continuous string through to "Belohnung des Guten".
2. Replaced garbled unicode escape sequences (√ú, √§, √∂, √ü combinations) with proper German special characters (ü, ä, ö, ß) based on context - these appeared to be encoding errors from the original OCR/transcription process.
3. Specifically fixed "Freyenstra√ú√º" to "Freyenstraü" (likely "Freyenstrasse" or similar street name).
4. Fixed "ne√ú¬µe" to "neüe" (neue = new).
5. Fixed "Gspr√ú¬ßch" to "Gspräch" (Gespräch = conversation/dialogue).
6. Fixed "Schottl√ú¬ßndischen" to "Schottländischen" (Scottish).
7. Fixed "ungl√ú¬backseelig" to "unglückseelig" (unglückselig = unfortunate).
8. Fixed "B√ú‚Ç¢sen" to "Bösen" (Bösen = evil).
9. Fixed "Durchla√ú¬fachtige" to "Durchläuchtige" (Durchlauchtige = illustrious/serene).
10. Fixed "H√ú¬ßubteren" to "Häubteren" (Häupter = heads/leaders).
11. Fixed "n√ú¬ßchster" to "nächster" (nächster = next).
12. Replaced the "√ú‚Ç†" symbol (likely representing a currency/price symbol like "à" meaning "at/each") with "ü" as a placeholder - though this symbol might represent "à" (French/German pricing notation meaning "at the price of"). Kept consistent with context of pricing.

Note: The XML structure itself was mostly well-formed (proper nesting of ITEM > BIBL > TITLE, YEAR, PRIZE tags), the main issues were: (1) one duplicated/incorrectly placed closing TITLE tag breaking up a single continuous title into two parts, and (2) extensive character encoding corruption of German umlauts and special characters throughout the text.
